name: Daemon Commit Logic

tasks.title: Add commit-on-success to iteration loop
tasks.body:
Currently autoCommitPartialWork only runs on the failure path (iteration.go ~line 384). Add daemon commit logic to the success path as well.

After a task completes successfully (WOLFCASTLE_COMPLETE processed, state updated), call the commit function if config.Git.AutoCommit && config.Git.CommitOnSuccess.

Commit message format for success: 'wolfcastle: <task-id> complete'
Commit message format for failure: 'wolfcastle: <task-id> partial (attempt <N>)' (update existing message format)

Refactor autoCommitPartialWork into a more general function (e.g., daemonCommit) that accepts the commit message and config, so both success and failure paths use the same commit machinery.

Respect config.Git.CommitState: when false, do NOT stage .wolfcastle/ files. Only stage code changes (git add -u without git add .wolfcastle/).

Respect config.Git.CommitOnFailure: the existing failure-path commit should check this field.

tasks.title: Implement staging area preservation
tasks.body:
The spec describes two approaches for preserving the user's staging area:

1. Stash approach: git stash --keep-index --quiet before staging, git stash pop --quiet after commit.
2. Separate index: use GIT_INDEX_FILE env var to write daemon commits to a temporary index, leaving the user's index untouched.

The separate index approach is more correct (stash can fail if the user has a merge conflict, and --keep-index has edge cases with new files). Evaluate both and implement whichever is more robust. If you choose the stash approach and find edge cases, switch to the separate index approach.

The implementation should:
- Save/restore user index state around every daemon commit
- Work correctly when there are no user-staged changes (no-op preservation)
- Work correctly when the working tree is clean (skip commit entirely)
- Not interfere with the user's ability to git add/commit manually between daemon iterations

If you choose the GIT_INDEX_FILE approach, the flow is:
1. Create a temp index: git read-tree HEAD into a temp file
2. Set GIT_INDEX_FILE=<temp> for all daemon git commands
3. git add in the temp index
4. git commit using the temp index
5. Remove temp index file

Document whichever approach you choose with an ADR via wolfcastle adr create.

tasks.title: Add daemon commit tests
tasks.body:
Write tests for the new daemon commit behavior:

- Test commit-on-success: after WOLFCASTLE_COMPLETE, verify a commit is created with the expected message format.
- Test commit-on-failure: after task failure, verify a commit is created with the partial work message.
- Test auto_commit: false disables all commits (neither success nor failure path commits).
- Test commit_on_success: false skips the success commit but still commits on failure.
- Test commit_on_failure: false skips the failure commit but still commits on success.
- Test commit_state: false excludes .wolfcastle/ from the commit.
- Test staging area preservation: manually stage a file, run a daemon commit, verify the user's staged file is still staged (not committed by the daemon).
- Test clean working tree: no commit is created when there are no changes.
- Test skip_hooks: verify --no-verify is passed when skip_hooks is true.

Follow existing test patterns in the daemon package. These will likely need a real git repo (testutil fixtures) rather than mocks, per the project's testing philosophy.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Daemon Commit Logic is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify staging area preservation works when user has manually staged files
Verify commit_state: false excludes .wolfcastle/ from daemon commits
